notes: First store opened in Tokyo (Ginza) in 1996 via a joint venture with Sazaby League; Japan became one of Starbucks' largest and most profitable markets outside North America.
Entered via Beijing in 1999; despite early skepticism about a tea-drinking culture China grew into Starbucks' second-largest market with continued aggressive expansion.
Entered Seoul in 1999; became one of Starbucks' strongest markets with dense store coverage and high sales per store.
Late entry into espresso's home market opening the Milan Reserve Roastery in September 2018; deliberately cautious premium positioning with a slow rollout and mixed reception.
Entered in 2012 through a 50/50 joint venture with Tata (Tata Starbucks); steady but capital-intensive growth in a tea-dominant price-sensitive market.
Entered Ho Chi Minh City in 2013; expansion stayed slow relative to neighboring markets given Vietnam's entrenched domestic coffee culture.
Aggressive company-owned expansion from 2000; heavy losses led to the 2008 closure of about two-thirds of its stores — a widely cited market-entry failure against established local cafe culture.
